contentBlocks.original:
शृणु नामानि जप्यानि केतो रथ महामते ।
केतुः स्थूलशिराश्चैव शिरोमात्रो ध्वजाकृतिः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śṛṇu nāmāni japyāni kētō ratha mahāmatē ।
kētuḥ sthūlaśirāśchaiva śirōmātrō dhvajākṛtiḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Listen to the names of Ketu to be recited: Ketu (the comet), Sthūlaśiras (of gross head), Śiromātra (only a head), Dhvajākṛti (of flag-like form). (1)

contentBlocks.original:
नवग्रहयुतः सिंहिकासुरीगर्भसम्भवः ।
महाभीतिकरश्चित्रवर्णो वै पिङ्गलाक्षकः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navagrahayutaḥ siṃhikāsurīgarbhasambhavaḥ ।
mahābhītikaraśchitravarṇō vai piṅgaḻākṣakaḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Navagrahayuta (joined to the nine planets), Siṃhikāsurīgarbhasambhava (born from the womb of the demoness Siṃhikā), Mahābhītikara (great fear-causer), Citravarṇa (of variegated colour), Piṅgaḷākṣaka (of tawny-yellow eyes). (2)

contentBlocks.original:
स फलोधूम्रसङ्काशः तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रो महोरगः ।
रक्तनेत्रश्चित्रकारी तीव्रकोपो महासुरः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa phalōdhūmrasaṅkāśaḥ tīkṣṇadaṃṣṭrō mahōragaḥ ।
raktanētraśchitrakārī tīvrakōpō mahāsuraḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Saphaḷōdhūmrasaṅkāśa (like dense dark smoke), Tīkṣṇadaṃṣṭra (sharp-fanged), Mahoraga (great serpent), Raktanetra (red-eyed), Citrakārī (the variegated doer), Tīvrakopa (of sharp anger), Mahāsura (great demon). (3)

contentBlocks.original:
क्रूरकण्ठः क्रोधनिधिश्छायाग्रहविशेषकः ।
अन्त्यग्रहो महाशीर्षो सूर्यारिः पुष्पवद्ग्रही ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
krūrakaṇṭhaḥ krōdhanidhiśChāyāgrahaviśēṣakaḥ ।
antyagrahō mahāśīrṣō sūryāriḥ puṣpavadgrahī ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Krūrakaṇṭha (of cruel throat), Krōdhinidhi (treasure of anger), Chāyāgrahaviśeṣaka (a special shadow-planet), Antyagraha (the last of the planets), Mahāśīrṣa (of great head), Sūryāri (enemy of the Sun), Puṣpavadgrahī (seizer of flower offerings). (4)

contentBlocks.original:
वरहस्तो गदापाणिश्चित्रवस्त्रधरस्तथा ।
चित्रध्वजपताकश्च घोरश्चित्ररथः शिखी ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
varahastō gadāpāṇiśchitravastradharastathā ।
chitradhvajapatākaścha ghōraśchitrarathaḥ śikhī ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Varahasta (boon-granting hand), Gadāpāṇi (mace in hand), Citravastradhara (wearer of variegated garments), Citradhvajapatāka (with variegated flag and banner), Ghora (terrible), Citraratha (of wonderful chariot), Śikhī (the crested one). (5)

contentBlocks.original:
कुलुत्थभक्षकश्चैव वैडूर्याभरणस्तथा ।
उत्पातजनकः शुक्रमित्रं मन्दसखस्तथा ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kuḻutthabhakṣakaśchaiva vaiḍūryābharaṇastathā ।
utpātajanakaḥ śukramitraṃ mandasakhastathā ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kuḷutthabhakṣaka (eater of horse-gram), Vaidūryābharaṇa (adorned with cat's-eye gems), Utpātajanaka (cause of portents), Śukramitra (friend of Venus), Mandasakha (companion of Saturn). (6)

contentBlocks.original:
गदाधरः नाकपतिः अन्तर्वेदीश्वरस्तथा ।
जैमिनीगोत्रजश्चित्रगुप्तात्मा दक्षिणामुखः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gadādharaḥ nākapatiḥ antarvēdīśvarastathā ।
jaiminīgōtrajaśchitraguptātmā dakṣiṇāmukhaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Gadādhara (mace-bearer), Nākapati (lord of heaven), Antarvediśvara (lord of the sacred enclosure), Jaiminīgōtraja (of the Jaiminī lineage), Citraguptātman (of the nature of Citragupta), Dakṣiṇāmukha (facing south). (7)

contentBlocks.original:
मुकुन्दवरपात्रं च महासुरकुलोद्भवः ।
घनवर्णो लम्बदेहो मृत्युपुत्रस्तथैव च ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mukundavarapātraṃ cha mahāsurakulōdbhavaḥ ।
ghanavarṇō lambadēhō mṛtyuputrastathaiva cha ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mukundavarapātra (recipient of Mukunda's boon), Mahāsurakulodbhava (of the lineage of great demons), Ghanavarṇa (of dense dark colour), Lambadēha (of elongated body), Mṛtyuputra (son of Death). (8)

contentBlocks.original:
उत्पातरूपधारी चाऽदृश्यः कालाग्निसन्निभः ।
नृपीडो ग्रहकारी च सर्वोपद्रवकारकः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
utpātarūpadhārī chā'dṛśyaḥ kālāgnisannibhaḥ ।
nṛpīḍō grahakārī cha sarvōpadravakārakaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Utpātarūpadhārī (one who takes the form of portents), Adṛśya (invisible), Kālāgnisannibha (resembling the fire of time), Nṛpīḍa (affliction of kings), Grahakārī (the planet-maker), Sarvōpadravakāraka (causer of all afflictions). (9)

contentBlocks.original:
चित्रप्रसूतो ह्यनलः सर्वव्याधिविनाशकः ।
अपसव्यप्रचारी च नवमे पापदायकः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chitraprasūtō hyanalaḥ sarvavyādhivināśakaḥ ।
apasavyaprachārī cha navamē pāpadāyakaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Citraprasūta (of wonderful birth), Anala (the fiery one), Sarvavyādhivināśaka (destroyer of all diseases), Apasavyapracārī (moving in the reverse direction); giver of sin when in the ninth house. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
पञ्चमे शोकदश्चोपरागखेचर एव च ।
अतिपुरुषकर्मा च तुरीये (तु) सुखप्रदः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pañchamē śōkadaśchōparāgakhēchara ēva cha ।
atipuruṣakarmā cha turīyē (tu) sukhapradaḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Giver of grief when in the fifth house; the eclipse-planet sky-mover; of supreme deeds (Atipuruṣakarmā); giver of happiness when in the fourth house. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
तृतीये वैरदः पापग्रहश्च स्फोटकारकः ।
प्राणनाथः पञ्चमे तु श्रमकारक एव च ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tṛtīyē vairadaḥ pāpagrahaścha sphōṭakārakaḥ ।
prāṇanāthaḥ pañchamē tu śramakāraka ēva cha ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Giver of enmity when in the third house; Pāpagraha (malefic planet); Sphoṭakāraka (cause of eruptions); Prāṇanātha (lord of life); cause of toil when in the fifth house. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
द्वितीयेऽस्फुटवाग्दाता विषाकुलितवक्त्रकः ।
कामरूपी सिंहदन्तः सत्येप्यनृतवानपि ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dvitīyē'sphuṭavāgdātā viṣākulitavaktrakaḥ ।
kāmarūpī siṃhadantaḥ satyēpyanṛtavānapi ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Giver of unclear speech when in the second house; Viṣākulitavaktraka (of poison-filled face); Kāmarūpī (taking any desired form); Siṃhadanta (of lion-like teeth); one who speaks falsehood even in truth. (13)

contentBlocks.original:
चतुर्थे मातृनाशश्च नवमे पितृनाशकः ।
अन्त्ये वैरप्रदश्चैव सुतानन्दनबन्धकः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturthē mātṛnāśaścha navamē pitṛnāśakaḥ ।
antyē vairapradaśchaiva sutānandanabandhakaḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Cause of the mother's loss when in the fourth; cause of the father's loss when in the ninth; giver of enmity at the end; a bond of joy and children. (14)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्पाक्षिजातोऽनङ्गश्च कर्मराश्युद्भवस्तथा ।
उपान्ते कीर्तिदश्चैव सप्तमे कलहप्रदः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarpākṣijātō'naṅgaścha karmarāśyudbhavastathā ।
upāntē kīrtidaśchaiva saptamē kalahapradaḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarpākṣijāta (born from the serpent's eye), Anaṅga (bodiless), Karmarāśyudbhava (born from Scorpio); giver of fame near the end; giver of strife when in the seventh house. (15)

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टमे व्याधिकर्ता च धने बहुसुखप्रदः ।
जनने रोगदश्चोर्ध्वमूर्धजो ग्रहनायकः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭamē vyādhikartā cha dhanē bahusukhapradaḥ ।
jananē rōgadaśchōrdhvamūrdhajō grahanāyakaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Disease-maker when in the eighth; giver of great happiness when in the second; disease-giver in the birth sign; Ūrdhvamūrdhaja (of upward-pointing hair); Grahanāyaka (leader of the planets). (16)

contentBlocks.original:
पापदृष्टिः खेचरश्च शाम्भवोऽशेषपूजितः ।
शाश्वतश्च नटश्चैव शुभाऽशुभफलप्रदः ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpadṛṣṭiḥ khēcharaścha śāmbhavō'śēṣapūjitaḥ ।
śāśvataścha naṭaśchaiva śubhā'śubhaphalapradaḥ ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Pāpadṛṣṭi (of sinful glance), Khēcara (sky-mover), Śāmbhava (of Śiva's nature), Aśeṣapūjita (worshipped by all), Śāśvata (eternal), Naṭa (the dancer / actor), Śubhāśubhaphalaprada (giver of both good and bad fruits). (17)

contentBlocks.original:
धूम्रश्चैव सुधापायी ह्यजितो भक्तवत्सलः ।
सिंहासनः केतुमूर्ती रवीन्दुद्युतिनाशकः ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhūmraśchaiva sudhāpāyī hyajitō bhaktavatsalaḥ ।
siṃhāsanaḥ kētumūrtī ravīndudyutināśakaḥ ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhūmra (smoky-hued), Sudhāpāyī (drinker of nectar), Ajita (unconquered), Bhaktavatsala (tender toward devotees), Siṃhāsana (of lion-throne), Ketumūrti (the embodiment of Ketu), Ravīndudyutināśaka (destroyer of the radiance of Sun and Moon). (18)

contentBlocks.original:
अमरः पीडकोऽमर्त्यो विष्णुदृष्टोऽसुरेश्वरः ।
भक्तरक्षोऽथ वैचित्र्यकपटस्यन्दनस्तथा ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amaraḥ pīḍakō'martyō viṣṇudṛṣṭō'surēśvaraḥ ।
bhaktarakṣō'tha vaichitryakapaṭasyandanastathā ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Amara (immortal), Pīḍaka (the afflicting one), Amartya (deathless), Viṣṇudṛṣṭa (beheld by Viṣṇu), Asureśvara (lord of the demons), Bhaktarakṣa (protector of devotees), Vaichitrya Kapaṭasyandana (of wonderful and deceitful chariot). (19)

contentBlocks.original:
विचित्रफलदायी च भक्ताभीष्टफलप्रदः ।
एतत्केतुग्रहस्योक्तं नाम्नामष्टोत्तरं शतम् ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vichitraphaladāyī cha bhaktābhīṣṭaphalapradaḥ ।
ētatkētugrahasyōktaṃ nāmnāmaṣṭōttaraṃ śatam ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vicitraphaladāyī (giver of wonderful fruits), Bhaktābhīṣṭaphalaprada (giver of all desired fruits to devotees). Thus these one hundred and eight names of the planet Ketu have been declared. (20)

contentBlocks.original:
यो भक्त्येदं जपेत्केतुर्नाम्नामष्टोत्तरं शतम् ।
स तु केतोः प्रसादेन सर्वाभीष्टं समाप्नुयात् ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yō bhaktyēdaṃ japētkēturnāmnāmaṣṭōttaraṃ śatam ।
sa tu kētōḥ prasādēna sarvābhīṣṭaṃ samāpnuyāt ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One who devoutly recites these one hundred and eight names of Ketu — that person, by the grace of Ketu, shall attain all that is desired. (21)

contentBlocks.original: इति श्री केतु अष्टोत्तरशतनाम स्तोत्रम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrī kētu aṣṭōttaraśatanāma stōtram ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Śrī Ketu Aṣṭottaraśatanāma Stotram.
